data.title: Morgan Stanley in charts: Institutional Securities Q1 revenues up ~30% Y/Y
Federal Realty Investment Trust to effect up to $300M share repurchase program
data.description:
Retail REIT Federal Realty Investment Trust (NYSE:FRT) said in a filing on Friday that its board of trustees have approved an up to $300M share repurchase program.

The company may purchase outstanding common shares of beneficial interest from time to time using a variety of methods, including open market, privately negotiated transactions or otherwise.

data.title: Aya Gold & Silver produces 1.07M oz of silver in Q1
data.description:
• Aya Gold & Silver (TSX:AYA:CA) announced on Friday Q1 silver production of 1.07M oz in Q1-2025 compared with 366,362 oz for the same period last year, representing a 192% increase.
  • The miner reported silver recovery of 82% in Q1-2025.
  • Combined mill availability of 91% in Q1-2025, in line with plans, said the company.
  • Mine production of 194,661t in Q1-2025 compared with 106,880t for the same period last year, representing an 82% increase, it added.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Valens Semiconductor rises after Needham begins coverage with bullish views
data.description:
Needham started coverage of Valens Semiconductor (NYSE:VLN) with a Buy rating and set a 12-month price target of $4 on the stock.

Shares of the Israeli company — which provides semiconductor products for the audio-video and automotive industries  — rose about 4% premarket on Friday.

Analysts led by N. Quinn Bolton said Valens is a fabless semiconductor company that designs connectivity semiconductor solutions for the Pro AV (Audio-Video), Automotive, Industrial, and Medical end markets. The company developed the interconnect technology underlying the HDBaseT and MIPI A-PHY standards and offers the longest reach USB 3.2 solutions in the market.

In ProAV, Valens dominates the high-end of the market with its HDBaseT solutions and is expanding into the mainstream segment with USB 3.2 (VS6320). In Automotive, three new MIPI A-PHY design wins should ramp in 2026, the analysts added.

Bolton and his team said that in the longer term, AI robotics and machine vision are catalystsdriving adoption in the Industrial and Medical segments. Valens' products will address a Total Addressable Market, or TAM, of more than $5B by 2028, up from around $2.6B today.

The analysts noted that Valens will continue to gain share in the high-end segment, steered by increasing adoption of its HDBaseT products and the end of inventory digestion. Additional share gains will be driven by the company's entry into the mainstream segment with its extended reach USB 3.2 (VS6320) solution, which has secured over 50 design wins that should begin to ramp in the second half of 2025.

data.title: Mandalay Resources reports Q1 2025 gold and antimony production
data.description:
• Mandalay Resources (TSX:MND:CA) announced on Friday the company produced a total of 20,376 ounces of gold and 161 tonnes of antimony representing a total of 22,342 gold equivalent ounces produced, compared to 22,346 ounces of gold and 404 tonnes of antimony in the first quarter of 2024, representing a total of 24,936 gold equivalent ounces produced.
  • The company sold a total of 23,386 ounces of gold and 196 tonnes of antimony, representing a total of 25,779 gold equivalent ounces, compared to 23,187 ounces of gold and 326 tonnes of antimony in the first quarter of 2024, representing a total of 25,277 gold equivalent ounces.
  • Frazer Bourchier, President and CEO, commented, “Production rates are expected to increase during the second half of the year, keeping the company on track to meet our annual production guidance of 85,000 – 95,000 gold equivalent ounces.”
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Eli Lilly to recognize $1.57B in Q1 as acquired R&D charges
data.description:
Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) announced Thursday that its financial results for Q1 2025, prepared under the U.S. generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP), will include $1.57B recognized on a pre-tax basis as acquired In-Process Research and Development (IPR&D) charges.

The acquired IPR&D charges are expected to impact the Indiana-based pharma giant’s earnings for the quarter, creating a headwind of about $1.57 per share for both GAAP and non-GAAP earnings.

The estimates are preliminary, as the company has yet to finalize its financials for the quarter ending Mar. 31, 2025.

According to Wall Street estimates, Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) is set to report $4.70 per share of earnings on average on revenue of $12.8B for Q1 2025.

Wall Street has remained bullish on Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) stock, with an average rating of Buy from analysts, in line with Seeking Alpha author ratings. However, SA’s Quant System, which consistently beats the market, rates LLY as a Hold.

data.title: Movado will restate certain financial statements due to misconduct at the Dubai branch of a subsidiary
data.description:
Movado Group (NYSE:MOV) disclosed on Friday that it became aware of allegations of misconduct within its Dubai branch of subsidiary, MGI Luxury Group Sárl that resulted in overstatement of sales, premature recognition of sales, and underreporting of credit notes owed to customers.

The actions of the former managing director and certain employees involved using an undisclosed third-party warehouse and falsifying documents to bypass internal controls, spanning approximately five years from fiscal year 2021. The investigation found no evidence of similar issues in other regions or involvement by employees outside the affected area. The managing director of the Dubai Branch has been terminated.

As a result of the incident, Movado determined that its consolidated financial statements for fiscal years 2022, 2023, and 2024, along with interim periods in fiscal years 2024 and 2025, require restatement to accurately reflect sales timing and credit issuance. The restated financials also include reduced operating expenses due to reversed accruals from adjusted operating results. Importantly, these misstatements did not affect the company's cash flows or compliance with debt covenants.

Movado (NYSE:MOV) said the investigation identified a material weakness in its internal controls over financial reporting. Specifically, the company’s risk assessment process failed to address risks tied to inadequate segregation of duties at the Dubai Branch. Management concluded that internal controls and disclosure procedures were ineffective during the affected periods. 

Movado (MOV) is scheduled to release earnings on April 16.

Shares of Movado Group (MOV) fell 4.9% in premarket trading on Friday, after shedding 4.7% on Thursday.
